metadata.title: Regulation (EC) No 1394/2007 of the European Parliament and of the Council on advanced therapy medicinal products and amending Directive 2001/83/EC and Regulation (EC) No 726/2004
metadata.document_type: EU Regulation — directly applicable in all Member States, applying from 30 December 2008
metadata.note: This is the foundational EU ATMP Regulation. No prior extraction found in output-v2/v3/v4. Covers definitions, marketing authorisation requirements, post-authorisation obligations, traceability, labelling, the Committee for Advanced Therapies (CAT), and procedural requirements. Note: This is the original 2007 text; subsequent amendments (e.g. by Regulation 2019/1243 omnibus regulation) are not reflected here — check for amendments when applying.

rules.rule_text: An 'advanced therapy medicinal product' (ATMP) means any of the following medicinal products for human use: (1) a gene therapy medicinal product as defined in Part IV of Annex I to Directive 2001/83/EC; (2) a somatic cell therapy medicinal product as defined in Part IV of Annex I to Directive 2001/83/EC; or (3) a tissue engineered product.
rules.verbatim_quote: 'Advanced therapy medicinal product' means any of the following medicinal products for human use: — a gene therapy medicinal product as defined in Part IV of Annex I to Directive 2001/83/EC, — a somatic cell therapy medicinal product as defined in Part IV of Annex I to Directive 2001/83/EC, — a tissue engineered product as defined in point (b).
rules.exceptions: ATMPs prepared on a non-routine basis per specific quality standards and used within the same Member State in a hospital under the exclusive professional responsibility of a medical practitioner, for compliance with an individual medical prescription for a custom-made product for an individual patient, are excluded from this Regulation's scope (hospital exemption — Article 28 amendment to Directive 2001/83/EC).

rules.rule_text: A 'tissue engineered product' is a product that (i) contains or consists of engineered cells or tissues, AND (ii) is presented as having properties for, or is used in or administered to human beings with a view to regenerating, repairing, or replacing a human tissue. Products containing exclusively non-viable human or animal cells/tissues which do not act principally by pharmacological, immunological, or metabolic action are excluded.
rules.verbatim_quote: 'Tissue engineered product' means a product that: — contains or consists of engineered cells or tissues, and — is presented as having properties for, or is used in or administered to human beings with a view to regenerating, repairing or replacing a human tissue. [...] Products containing or consisting exclusively of non-viable human or animal cells and/or tissues, which do not contain any viable cells or tissues and which do not act principally by pharmacological, immunological or metabolic action, shall be excluded from this definition.
rules.exceptions: Non-viable cells/tissues acting primarily by physical means cannot be ATMPs by definition.

rules.rule_text: Cells or tissues shall be considered 'engineered' if they fulfil at least ONE of the following conditions: (a) the cells or tissues have been subject to substantial manipulation, so that biological characteristics, physiological functions, or structural properties relevant for the intended regeneration, repair, or replacement are achieved; OR (b) the cells or tissues are not intended to be used for the same essential function(s) in the recipient as in the donor. The manipulations listed in Annex I (non-substantial manipulations) shall NOT be considered substantial manipulations.
rules.verbatim_quote: Cells or tissues shall be considered 'engineered' if they fulfil at least one of the following conditions: — the cells or tissues have been subject to substantial manipulation, so that biological characteristics, physiological functions or structural properties relevant for the intended regeneration, repair or replacement are achieved. The manipulations listed in Annex I, in particular, shall not be considered as substantial manipulations, — the cells or tissues are not intended to be used for the same essential function or functions in the recipient as in the donor.
rules.exceptions: Annex I lists non-substantial manipulations: cutting, grinding, shaping, centrifugation, soaking in antibiotic/antimicrobial solutions, sterilization, irradiation, cell separation/concentration/purification, filtering, lyophilization, freezing, cryopreservation, vitrification.

rules.rule_text: A 'combined advanced therapy medicinal product' means an ATMP that incorporates, as an integral part of the product, one or more medical devices (Directive 93/42/EEC) or one or more active implantable medical devices (Directive 90/385/EEC), AND its cellular or tissue part must contain viable cells or tissues OR its cellular/tissue part containing non-viable cells/tissues must be liable to act upon the human body with action considered primary to that of the devices.
rules.verbatim_quote: 'Combined advanced therapy medicinal product' means an advanced therapy medicinal product that fulfils the following conditions: — it must incorporate, as an integral part of the product, one or more medical devices within the meaning of Article 1(2)(a) of Directive 93/42/EEC or one or more active implantable medical devices within the meaning of Article 1(2)(c) of Directive 90/385/EEC, and — its cellular or tissue part must contain viable cells or tissues, or — its cellular or tissue part containing non-viable cells or tissues must be liable to act upon the human body with action that can be considered as primary to that of the devices referred to.

rules.rule_text: Where a product contains viable cells or tissues, the pharmacological, immunological, or metabolic action of those cells or tissues shall be considered as the principal mode of action of the product for regulatory classification purposes.
rules.verbatim_quote: Where a product contains viable cells or tissues, the pharmacological, immunological or metabolic action of those cells or tissues shall be considered as the principal mode of action of the product.

rules.rule_text: An ATMP containing both autologous (from the patient) and allogeneic (from another human) cells or tissues shall be considered to be for allogeneic use.
rules.verbatim_quote: An advanced therapy medicinal product containing both autologous (emanating from the patient himself) and allogeneic (coming from another human being) cells or tissues shall be considered to be for allogeneic use.

rules.rule_text: A product that may fall within the definition of both a somatic cell therapy medicinal product/tissue engineered product AND a gene therapy medicinal product shall be considered as a gene therapy medicinal product.
rules.verbatim_quote: A product which may fall within the definition of: — a somatic cell therapy medicinal product or a tissue engineered product, and — a gene therapy medicinal product, shall be considered as a gene therapy medicinal product.

rules.rule_text: Where an ATMP contains human cells or tissues, the donation, procurement, and testing of those cells or tissues must be made in accordance with Directive 2004/23/EC.
rules.verbatim_quote: Where an advanced therapy medicinal product contains human cells or tissues, the donation, procurement and testing of those cells or tissues shall be made in accordance with Directive 2004/23/EC.
rules.exceptions: Directive 2004/23/EC applies only to donation, procurement, and testing; further aspects (manufacturing, etc.) are covered by this Regulation.

rules.rule_text: The manufacture of ATMPs must be in compliance with GMP principles per Commission Directive 2003/94/EC, adapted, where necessary, to reflect the specific nature of ATMP manufacturing. GMP guidelines specific to ATMPs must be drawn up by the Commission after consulting the Agency.
rules.verbatim_quote: The Commission shall, after consulting the Agency, draw up guidelines in line with the principles of good manufacturing practice and specific to advanced therapy medicinal products. [...] The manufacture of advanced therapy medicinal products should be in compliance with the principles of good manufacturing practice, as set out in Commission Directive 2003/94/EC [...] and adapted, where necessary, to reflect the specific nature of those products.

rules.rule_text: A medical device which forms part of a combined ATMP must meet the essential requirements laid down in Annex I to Directive 93/42/EEC. An active implantable medical device forming part of a combined ATMP must meet the essential requirements laid down in Annex I to Directive 90/385/EEC.
rules.source_section: Article 6 — Issues specific to medical devices
rules.verbatim_quote: A medical device which forms part of a combined advanced therapy medicinal product shall meet the essential requirements laid down in Annex I to Directive 93/42/EEC. An active implantable medical device which forms part of a combined advanced therapy medicinal product shall meet the essential requirements laid down in Annex I to Directive 90/385/EEC.

rules.rule_text: Applications for marketing authorisation of an ATMP containing medical devices, bio-materials, scaffolds, or matrices must include a description of the physical characteristics and performance of the product and a description of the product design methods, in accordance with Annex I to Directive 2001/83/EC.
rules.source_section: Article 7 — Specific requirements for ATMPs containing devices
rules.verbatim_quote: In addition to the requirements laid down in Article 6(1) of Regulation (EC) No 726/2004, applications for the authorisation of an advanced therapy medicinal product containing medical devices, bio-materials, scaffolds or matrices shall include a description of the physical characteristics and performance of the product and a description of the product design methods, in accordance with Annex I to Directive 2001/83/EC.

rules.rule_text: The Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use (CHMP) shall consult the Committee for Advanced Therapies (CAT) on any scientific assessment of ATMPs. When preparing a draft opinion, the CAT shall endeavour to reach a scientific consensus; if not possible, the CAT shall adopt the majority position and mention divergent positions and their grounds.
rules.verbatim_quote: The Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use shall consult the Committee for Advanced Therapies on any scientific assessment of advanced therapy medicinal products necessary to draw up the scientific opinions referred to in Article 5(2) and (3) of Regulation (EC) No 726/2004. [...] When preparing a draft opinion for final approval by the Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use, the Committee for Advanced Therapies shall endeavour to reach a scientific consensus. If such consensus cannot be reached, the Committee for Advanced Therapies shall adopt the position of the majority of its members.
rules.exceptions: Where CHMP scientific opinion is not in accordance with CAT draft opinion, CHMP shall annex to its opinion a detailed explanation of the scientific grounds for the differences.

rules.rule_text: For combined ATMPs: (1) the whole product shall be subject to final evaluation by the Agency; (2) the MAA must include evidence of conformity with essential requirements for the device component (Article 6); and (3) where available, the MAA must include results of assessment of the medical device/active implantable device part by a notified body.
rules.source_section: Article 9 — Combined advanced therapy medicinal products
rules.verbatim_quote: Where a combined advanced therapy medicinal product is concerned, the whole product shall be subject to final evaluation by the Agency. The application for a marketing authorisation for a combined advanced therapy medicinal product shall include evidence of conformity with the essential requirements referred to in Article 6. The application for a marketing authorisation for a combined advanced therapy medicinal product shall include, where available, the results of the assessment by a notified body in accordance with Directive 93/42/EEC or Directive 90/385/EEC of the medical device part or active implantable medical device part.
rules.exceptions: If application does not include notified body assessment results, the Agency shall seek an opinion on conformity of the device part unless the CAT decides involvement of a notified body is not required.

rules.rule_text: The Summary of Product Characteristics (SmPC) for an ATMP must contain the information listed in Annex II to this Regulation, in the order indicated therein. Where the product contains cells or tissues, the SmPC must include a detailed description of those cells/tissues and their specific origin, including the species of animal in cases of non-human origin.
rules.source_section: Article 10 — Summary of product characteristics; Annex II
rules.verbatim_quote: By way of derogation from Article 11 of Directive 2001/83/EC, the summary of the product characteristics for advanced therapy medicinal products shall contain the information listed in Annex II to this Regulation, in the order indicated therein. [...] qualitative and quantitative composition in terms of the active substances and other constituents of the product, knowledge of which is essential for proper use, administration or implantation of the product. Where the product contains cells or tissues, a detailed description of these cells or tissues and of their specific origin, including the species of animal in cases of non-human origin, shall be provided.

rules.rule_text: The outer packaging (or, where there is no outer packaging, the immediate packaging) of ATMPs must display the particulars listed in Annex III to this Regulation. Where the product contains cells or tissues, the packaging must state 'This product contains cells of human/animal [as appropriate] origin' together with a short description and specific origin.
rules.source_section: Article 11 — Labelling of outer/immediate packaging; Annex III
rules.verbatim_quote: By way of derogation from Articles 54 and 55(1) of Directive 2001/83/EC, the particulars listed in Annex III to this Regulation shall appear on the outer packaging of advanced therapy medicinal products or, where there is no outer packaging, on the immediate packaging. [...] A description of the active substance(s) expressed qualitatively and quantitatively, including, where the product contains cells or tissues, the statement 'This product contains cells of human/animal [as appropriate{]} origin' together with a short description of these cells or tissues and of their specific origin.
rules.exceptions: Derogates from Articles 54 and 55(1) of Directive 2001/83/EC.

rules.rule_text: The immediate packaging of ATMPs must additionally display: (a) the unique donation and product codes as referred to in Article 8(2) of Directive 2004/23/EC; and (b) for ATMPs for autologous use: the unique patient identifier and the statement 'For autologous use only'.
rules.verbatim_quote: In addition to the particulars mentioned in Article 55(2) and (3) of Directive 2001/83/EC, the following particulars shall appear on the immediate packaging of advanced therapy medicinal products: (a) the unique donation and product codes, as referred to in Article 8(2) of Directive 2004/23/EC; (b) in the case of advanced therapy medicinal products for autologous use, the unique patient identifier and the statement 'For autologous use only'.

rules.rule_text: The package leaflet for an ATMP must be drawn up in accordance with the SmPC and include the information listed in Annex IV to this Regulation, in the order indicated therein. The package leaflet must reflect the results of consultations with target patient groups to ensure legibility, clarity, and ease of use.
rules.verbatim_quote: By way of derogation from Article 59(1) of Directive 2001/83/EC, the package leaflet for an advanced therapy medicinal product shall be drawn up in accordance with the summary of product characteristics and shall include the information listed in Annex IV to this Regulation, in the order indicated therein. The package leaflet shall reflect the results of consultations with target patient groups to ensure that it is legible, clear and easy to use.

rules.rule_text: The applicant must detail in the marketing authorisation application the measures envisaged to ensure follow-up of the efficacy of advanced therapy medicinal products and of adverse reactions thereto.
rules.source_section: Article 14(1) — Post-authorisation follow-up of efficacy and adverse reactions, and risk management
rules.verbatim_quote: In addition to the requirements for pharmacovigilance laid down in Articles 21 to 29 of Regulation (EC) No 726/2004, the applicant shall detail, in the marketing authorisation application, the measures envisaged to ensure the follow-up of efficacy of advanced therapy medicinal products and of adverse reactions thereto.

rules.rule_text: Where there is particular cause for concern, the Commission shall, on the advice of the Agency, require as part of the marketing authorisation that a risk management system designed to identify, characterise, prevent, or minimise risks related to ATMPs, including an evaluation of the effectiveness of that system, be set up, or that specific post-marketing studies be carried out by the MAH and submitted to the Agency.
rules.verbatim_quote: Where there is particular cause for concern, the Commission shall, on the advice of the Agency, require as part of the marketing authorisation that a risk management system designed to identify, characterise, prevent or minimise risks related to advanced therapy medicinal products, including an evaluation of the effectiveness of that system, be set up, or that specific post-marketing studies be carried out by the holder of the marketing authorisation and submitted for review to the Agency.

rules.rule_text: The marketing authorisation holder of an ATMP must establish and maintain a system ensuring that the individual product and its starting and raw materials, including all substances coming into contact with cells or tissues, can be traced through sourcing, manufacturing, packaging, storage, transport, and delivery to the hospital, institution, or private practice where the product is used.
rules.verbatim_quote: The holder of a marketing authorisation for an advanced therapy medicinal product shall establish and maintain a system ensuring that the individual product and its starting and raw materials, including all substances coming into contact with the cells or tissues it may contain, can be traced through the sourcing, manufacturing, packaging, storage, transport and delivery to the hospital, institution or private practice where the product is used.

rules.rule_text: The hospital, institution, or private practice where the ATMP is used must establish and maintain a system for patient and product traceability, containing sufficient detail to allow linking of each product to the patient who received it and vice versa.
rules.verbatim_quote: The hospital, institution or private practice where the advanced therapy medicinal product is used shall establish and maintain a system for patient and product traceability. That system shall contain sufficient detail to allow linking of each product to the patient who received it and vice versa.

rules.rule_text: Where an ATMP contains human cells or tissues, the MAH, as well as the hospital/institution/private practice where the product is used, must ensure that the traceability systems comply with and are compatible with the requirements of Directives 2004/23/EC (human tissues and cells) and 2002/98/EC (human blood cells).
rules.verbatim_quote: Where an advanced therapy medicinal product contains human cells or tissues, the marketing authorisation holder, as well as the hospital, institution or private practice where the product is used, shall ensure that the traceability systems established in accordance with paragraphs 1 and 2 of this Article are complementary to, and compatible with, the requirements laid down in Articles 8 and 14 of Directive 2004/23/EC as regards human cells and tissues other than blood cells, and Articles 14 and 24 of Directive 2002/98/EC as regards human blood cells.

rules.rule_text: The marketing authorisation holder must keep traceability data for a minimum of 30 years after the expiry date of the product, or longer if required by the Commission as a term of the marketing authorisation. In case of bankruptcy or liquidation of the MAH, if the MA is not transferred to another legal entity, the traceability data must be transferred to the Agency.
rules.verbatim_quote: The marketing authorisation holder shall keep the data referred to in paragraph 1 for a minimum of 30 years after the expiry date of the product, or longer if required by the Commission as a term of the marketing authorisation. In case of bankruptcy or liquidation of the marketing authorisation holder, and in the event that the marketing authorisation is not transferred to another legal entity, the data referred to in paragraph 1 shall be transferred to the Agency.
rules.exceptions: Commission may extend the retention period beyond 30 years as a condition of the MA.

rules.rule_text: If the marketing authorisation for an ATMP is suspended, revoked, or withdrawn, the MAH remains subject to the traceability obligations laid down in Article 15(1), (3), and (4) (establishment of traceability system, compatibility with cell/tissue directives, and 30-year data retention).
rules.verbatim_quote: In the event that the marketing authorisation is suspended, revoked or withdrawn, the holder of the marketing authorisation shall remain subject to the obligations laid down in paragraphs 1, 3 and 4.

rules.rule_text: Any applicant or holder of a marketing authorisation may request scientific advice from the Agency on the design and conduct of pharmacovigilance and the risk management system. For SMEs, the Agency shall apply a 90% reduction on fees for scientific advice on ATMPs; for other applicants, a 65% reduction applies.
rules.verbatim_quote: The applicant or holder of a marketing authorisation may request advice from the Agency on the design and conduct of pharmacovigilance and of the risk management system referred to in Article 14. [...] By way of derogation from Article 8(1) of Council Regulation (EC) No 297/95 [...] a 90 % reduction for small and medium-sized enterprises and 65 % for other applicants shall apply to the fee for scientific advice payable to the Agency for any advice given in respect of advanced therapy medicinal products.

rules.rule_text: Any applicant developing a product based on genes, cells, or tissues may request a scientific recommendation from the Agency on whether the product falls, on scientific grounds, within the definition of an ATMP. The Agency must deliver this recommendation within 60 days of receipt of the request. The Agency shall publish summaries of recommendations delivered (after deletion of commercially confidential information).
rules.source_section: Article 17 — Scientific recommendation on advanced therapy classification
rules.verbatim_quote: Any applicant developing a product based on genes, cells or tissues may request a scientific recommendation of the Agency with a view to determining whether the referred product falls, on scientific grounds, within the definition of an advanced therapy medicinal product. The Agency shall deliver this recommendation after consultation with the Commission and within 60 days after receipt of the request. The Agency shall publish summaries of the recommendations delivered in accordance with paragraph 1, after deletion of all information of commercial confidential nature.

rules.rule_text: Small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) developing an ATMP may submit all relevant quality data and, where available, non-clinical data to the Agency for scientific evaluation and certification, independently of any marketing authorisation application. This certification facilitates future clinical trial and MAA evaluation based on the same data.
rules.source_section: Article 18 — Certification of quality and non-clinical data
rules.verbatim_quote: Small and medium-sized enterprises developing an advanced therapy medicinal product may submit to the Agency all relevant quality and, where available, non-clinical data required in accordance with modules 3 and 4 of Annex I to Directive 2001/83/EC, for scientific evaluation and certification.
rules.exceptions: Certification is not legally binding but aims to facilitate evaluation of future MAA.

rules.rule_text: The fee for marketing authorisation shall be reduced by 50% if the applicant is a hospital or an SME and can prove there is a particular public health interest in the Community in the advanced therapy medicinal product concerned.
rules.source_section: Article 19 — Reduction of the fee for marketing authorisation
rules.verbatim_quote: By way of derogation from Regulation (EC) No 297/95, the fee for marketing authorisation shall be reduced by 50 % if the applicant is a hospital or a small or medium-sized enterprise and can prove that there is a particular public health interest in the Community in the advanced therapy medicinal product concerned.
rules.exceptions: Fee waiver applies to authorisation applications only; post-authorisation activities in the first year also covered by Article 19(2).

rules.rule_text: Members and alternates of the Committee for Advanced Therapies (CAT) shall have no financial or other interests in the biotechnology sector and medical device sector that could affect their impartiality. All indirect interests that could relate to these sectors shall be entered in the Agency's register of interests per Article 63(2) of Regulation (EC) No 726/2004.
rules.verbatim_quote: In addition to the requirements laid down in Article 63 of Regulation (EC) No 726/2004, members and alternates of the Committee for Advanced Therapies shall have no financial or other interests in the biotechnology sector and medical device sector that could affect their impartiality. All indirect interests that could relate to these sectors shall be entered in the register referred to in Article 63(2) of Regulation (EC) No 726/2004.

rules.rule_text: ATMPs (other than tissue engineered products) that were legally on the Community market on 30 December 2008 must comply with this Regulation no later than 30 December 2011. Tissue engineered products legally on the market on 30 December 2008 must comply no later than 30 December 2012. No fees are payable to the Agency for authorisation applications of these transitional products.
rules.verbatim_quote: Advanced therapy medicinal products, other than tissue engineered products, which were legally on the Community market in accordance with national or Community legislation on 30 December 2008, shall comply with this Regulation no later than 30 December 2011. Tissue engineered products which were legally on the Community market in accordance with national or Community legislation on 30 December 2008 shall comply with this Regulation no later than 30 December 2012. By way of derogation from Article 3(1) of Regulation (EC) No 297/95, no fee shall be payable to the Agency in respect of applications submitted for the authorisation of the advanced therapy medicinal products mentioned in paragraphs 1 and 2 of this Article.
rules.exceptions: Transitional products benefit from fee waiver for authorisation applications.
cross_references: Article 30 — Entry into force (applies from 30 December 2008)
